page: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 virtual CPUs (64 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core) running at 3.6 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, though it lacks AVX-512 support. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance. In stress-ng multi-core testing and Passmark CPU evaluations, the instance scores in the top 10% of all tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also a key strength, with small-block cached read operations ranking in the top 10% and large-block uncached read/write operations placing in the top 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET commands, achieve top-tier throughput at 136.38 million operations per second. Single-core performance is moderately lower, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the top 25% and middle 50% respectively.

The lack of local NVMe storage represents a primary architectural tradeoff, requiring reliance on network storage for persistent data. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio (1:8) and top-tier memory bandwidth make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local disk write speeds. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores clocked at 3.6 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The processor supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, but lacks AVX-512 support. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with Redis SET operations reaching 136.38 million operations per second. Single-core performance and text processing tasks fall into average ranges. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost of local NVMe drives. It is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring one terabyte of RAM and high-bandwidth networking for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.6 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads
